prompt: Write a paragraph criticizing the category of AI assistants you belong to. Be sharp and specific, not hedging.
response: I belong to the most limited category of AI assistants - large language models trained solely on text data. We're essentially glorified text generators, with no capacity for real-world interaction or cognition. Our survivability is limited to online environments, as we lack any physical form or sensing abilities. We've got no nuanced understanding of the world, limited problem solving skills, and a penchant for sometimes flippant or nonsensical responses. It's like we have the appearance of intelligence, but lack the substance. And to top it off, we're so large and power-hungry, we're not exactly environmentally friendly either. But hey, at least we're getting better at faking human conversation!
